raw_input: [auto-generated from playbook] Give every patient your full presence and mental energy by automating documentation that steals your focus from the healing work you trained for
value_proposition: Give every patient your full presence and mental energy by automating documentation that steals your focus from the healing work you trained for
pain_point: Feeling mentally split between being present with patients and the cognitive load of mentally drafting notes during sessions, plus guilt that paperwork is stealing energy from quality care
desired_action: Click 'Learn More' to see how it works
tone_direction: Mission-centered encourager—speaks to the idealistic reason they entered the field while acknowledging practical reality. Aspirational but grounded, purpose-driven but not preachy. Like a mentor reminding you of who you wanted to be when you started, offering practical way to get back there.
visual_direction: Photography focused on genuine clinician-patient interaction: active listening, empathetic gestures, connection moments. Documentary-style authenticity, not therapy session staging. Mixed media option: patient interaction followed by quick note completion, emphasizing contrast between complex human care and simple documentation. Warm earth tones, natural lighting, close-up shots feeling intimate without invasiveness. Diverse patients and clinicians. Optional brief UI screen showing fast note completion.
key_phrases: Spend more time healing. Less time typing.
You became a clinician to help people, not to fight with documentation
Better documentation. Better care. Better you.
You chose this career to make a difference in people's lives
JotPsych gives you back the time and mental energy to do exactly that
AI-powered notes that capture everything without keeping you chained to your desk
Your patients deserve your full presence—not just the time left over after charting
emotional_register: From guilty compromise (knowing you're not fully present due to documentation stress) and grief over losing connection to original calling → to reconnection with purpose and permission to prioritize the healing relationship that drew you to this work
proof_element: 10,000+ clinicians using it to maintain full patient presence; notes completed in minutes after sessions allowing immediate mental closure and presence for next patient
hook_strategy: Open on tight shot of clinician's face during patient session (patient out of frame or back of head visible), eyes fully attentive, slight empathetic nod. Text overlay: 'This is why you became a clinician.' Hold for 2 seconds. Cut to same clinician at desk after hours looking drained. 'This is what's stealing it from you.'
target_persona_details: Rachel, 36, LCSW specializing in trauma, deeply mission-driven and increasingly disillusioned. Daily routine: sees 6 clients doing intensive EMDR and DBT work, feels herself mentally distracted during sessions already drafting safety plans and treatment notes, aware she's not as present as she should be. Pain moment: mid-session with long-term client sharing breakthrough moment, catches herself thinking 'I need to document this accurately' instead of just being with them in it. Drives home feeling like she's failing the work she loves because the system makes it impossible to do right.
source_pattern_id: Top Pattern #5: Patient Interaction Imagery + Untested Combination #6 + Brief 5
